target.name: Phasecraft

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Phasecraft is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Montanaro, Cubitt, and Morton, having raised $25M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 identifiable peers—3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private entities including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion—though comparable valuations at Phasecraft's specific stage and tier remain sparse.

Only one valuation method proved applicable given data constraints. Stage-progression analysis, using the $25M raised as a value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implies a $50M valuation. This reflects normalized growth expectations between Series A and subsequent funding rounds. Peer-median triangulation was not feasible; while 7 quantum computing peers exist in the broader universe, none satisfied minimum cohort requirements (three or more firms matching stage, proximity, or valuation tier simultaneously), making sector-wide median analysis unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, centered at $50M. This range reflects upside and downside variance around the stage-progression point estimate but is constrained by reliance on a single method. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct same-stage comparables, which limits benchmarking precision, and the nascent, heterogeneous nature of quantum computing commercialization models. Additional fundraising activity or revised peer financials would strengthen conviction.
